parsed.answer: Many cities receive more annual rainfall than Seattle (~37-38 inches/year). US examples: New Orleans (~64 in), Miami (~62 in), Mobile AL (~67 in), Houston (~50 in), Atlanta (~50 in), Jacksonville (~52 in), New York City (~47 in), Boston (~43 in). Internationally: Singapore (~90 in), Mumbai during monsoon season, Bergen Norway, and Cherrapunji India (one of the wettest places on Earth). Seattle's rainy reputation comes from its high number of overcast/drizzly days, not from high total precipitation.
